summary: Unmerged Corneria stage decompilation work that implemented a batch of grcorneria.c functions and supporting type/header changes. The author explicitly opened it as a progress/reference PR that would be superseded by another PR by nemo. The decomp.dev bot reported one new full match, grCorneria_801E2110, and many near-matches for Corneria stage behavior, arwing/platform movement, stage section selection, color setup, and conversion/joint setup routines.
what_changed: Replaced many grcorneria.c nonmatching placeholders with C implementations, including grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DD350, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801DFC98, grCorneria_801DFF20, grCorneria_801E01A8, grCorneria_801E0678, grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E2110, grCorneria_801E2228, and grCorneria_801E25C4.

Added local Corneria helper/data structs in grcorneria.c: grCn_Entry, grCn_JointPair, grCn_ConvData, and a more detailed grCn_Data layout with x30, indices, x48, positions, joint_pairs, and entries fields.
Added static globals grCn_804D69A8 and grCn_804D69B0, plus static sdata color constants grCn_804DB218 through grCn_804DB238 used by grCorneria_801E2110.
Included lb/lbaudio_ax.h and used lbAudioAx_800237A8 for Corneria arwing/audio state transitions in grCorneria_801DDE88.

Updated grcorneria.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to known signatures for several functions, including grCorneria_801DCCFC returning s32, grCorneria_801DDE88 taking HSD_GObj* and returning s32, grCorneria_801E03C8 taking Ground_GObj* and s32, and grCorneria_801E08CC returning s32.
Expanded struct grArwing_GroundVars in src/melee/gr/types.h with byte/bitfield views of xC4, xC6, and xE0, and changed xD4 and xD8 from f32 to s32 based on observed usage.

Added target_funcs.txt listing completed and remaining grcorneria.c functions, marking 14 functions checked off and leaving items such as grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801E1970, grCorneria_801E03C8, grCorneria_801E277C, and grCorneria_801DED50 unchecked.
Adjusted one existing PAD_STACK usage in grCorneria_801E1878 from PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12), indicating stack-layout tuning for matching.
smart_moves: Pulled repeated offset access into named local structs for Corneria data tables and conversion state instead of leaving every access as opaque pointer arithmetic.

Modeled arwing ground variables with unions and bitfields so code could express flag manipulation such as xC4_flags_b1, xC4_flags_b2, xC6_flags_b0, and xE0_flags_b0 while preserving the underlying packed layout.
Kept M2C_FIELD and raw byte accesses where the struct knowledge was still incomplete, which allowed progress without overcommitting uncertain field names or types.
Used explicit static color constants in .sdata for grCorneria_801E2110, which produced the only fully matched new function reported by the bot.
Captured remaining function status in target_funcs.txt, making the PR useful as a handoff/reference despite being superseded.
Updated public prototypes when return types and parameters became known, reducing downstream UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS usage.
decomp_lessons: For stage files with many related callbacks, partial local structs can unlock many functions at once, but uncertain fields should remain conservatively named or accessed with M2C_FIELD until verified.
Ground variable unions are useful when a field is used both as a whole word and as packed flag bytes; this PR demonstrates that bitfield views can make decompiled stage logic clearer.
Near-matches can still be valuable handoff material when a superseding PR is expected, especially if the PR documents which functions were attempted and what match percentages were achieved.

Bot match reports should be read carefully: this PR added substantial source but only one new function reached 100%, while many others remained around 93-99% and therefore still needed matching cleanup.
Header signature cleanup should accompany function decompilation so future work can reuse typed calls rather than propagating unknown prototypes.
naming_conventions: Corneria file-local symbols use the grCn_ prefix for data and static globals, for example grCn_803E1D38, grCn_803E2204, grCn_804D69A8, and grCn_804DB218.
Corneria functions retain address-based names with the grCorneria_ prefix, such as grCorneria_801E08CC and grCorneria_801E2110.
New temporary structs use address/domain-based names rather than semantic final names when meaning is uncertain: grCn_Entry, grCn_JointPair, grCn_ConvData, and grCn_Data.
Unknown or partially understood fields are named by offset, such as xC4, xC8, xD4, xD8, xE0, x30, x48, xC, x10, x14, and x1C.
Packed flag bitfields are named with field offset and bit index, for example xC4_flags_b0 through xC4_flags_b7, xC6_flags_b0 through xC6_flags_b7, and xE0_flags_b0 through xE0_flags_b7.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with sizes such as 8, 12, 16, and 32 to influence stack layout and matching.
Used M2C_FIELD for still-unknown data at fixed offsets in grCn_804D69A0 and grCn_Data, including offsets such as 0x3C, 0x40, 0x70, 0x88, and computed table access near 0x3D8.
Used raw u8* casts of Ground data to test and write byte offsets such as 0xC4, 0xC5, 0xC6, and 0xE0 when the precise typed layout was not fully settled.
Used explicit casts between Ground_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, Ground*, HSD_JObj*, GXColor*, and raw scalar pointers to mirror the original code's memory interpretation.

Used static s32 constants for packed GXColor values, then passed their stack copies as GXColor* to Ground_801C058C, Ground_801C05EC, Ground_801C05A4, Ground_801C055C, Ground_801C05BC, and Ground_801C0574.

Matched switch/state-machine style logic for arwing behavior, including xD8 states in grCorneria_801DDE88 and xC5 stage/platform movement states in grCorneria_801DFC98, grCorneria_801DFF20, and grCorneria_801E01A8.
Relied on HSD_JObj translation and scale helpers, Ground_801C2BA4/Ground_801C3FA4 lookups, Camera_GetTransformInterest, HSD_Randi, and lbAudioAx_800237A8 to reconstruct stage behavior.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the supplied slice.
Author note: the PR would be superseded by another PR by nemo and was opened to track progress and preserve anything useful.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code changed to 61.73%, +0.01% and +280 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 1 new full match: main/melee/gr/grcorneria grCorneria_801E2110, +280 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.

decomp.dev bot reported 13 unmatched-item improvements, including grCorneria_801E0678 at 99.73%, grCorneria_801E2228 at 99.93%, grCorneria_801DFC98/grCorneria_801DFF20/grCorneria_801E01A8 at 99.85%, grCorneria_801DDAC4 at 98.28%, grCorneria_801E08CC at 99.42%, grCorneria_801DDE88 at 97.77%, grCorneria_801E25C4 at 93.55%, grCorneria_801DD350 at 99.88%, and grCorneria_801DCCFC at 95.69%.
decomp.dev bot also reported a .sdata regression from 70.97% to 51.16%, so the added static data was not fully aligned with existing data matching.
follow_up_queries: Find the superseding nemo Corneria PR and compare which near-matches from PR #2382 were reused or replaced.
Search later grcorneria.c history for grCorneria_801E2110 to see whether the static GXColor constant approach survived review.
Check subsequent changes to grArwing_GroundVars to validate whether xD4/xD8 as s32 and the xC4/xC6/xE0 bitfield layout were retained.
Investigate unresolved target_funcs.txt entries from this PR, especially grCorneria_801E03C8, grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801E1970, and grCorneria_801DED50.
Review later .sdata/.sdata2 matching changes for grcorneria to understand and fix the bot-reported .sdata regression.
